_comment: Fixture stem -> the request shape of a JWT-protected endpoint the harness fires tokens at: the protected path, the auth header + scheme, the expected audience/issuer the verifier enforces, and the subject to mint into the valid token. A JWT-auth test is about VERIFIER SECURITY (which tokens are accepted/rejected), not the response body shape, so the bundled fixtures are REQUEST/CLAIM TEMPLATES: hs256_bearer matches the bundled correct/naive stubs (the default), and hs256_no_claims shows an endpoint that does not enforce aud/iss (so the audience-enforced / issuer-enforced properties SKIP rather than fail). The signing secret and the RSA public-key blob are TEST material shipped in this directory (clearly labelled, never a real secret) — see PROVENANCE.md. To test your own API, edit expected_aud/expected_iss/path to match and pass --secret (and --pubkey) on the command line.

hs256_bearer.subject: jatk-demo-user-001
hs256_bearer.note: primary — matches the bundled stubs (HS256 Bearer, aud + iss enforced)

hs256_no_claims.subject: jatk-demo-user-001
hs256_no_claims.note: an endpoint that does NOT enforce aud/iss — the audience/issuer properties SKIP (not fail)
